21.correct: Fundamental Duties were added to the Indian Constitution by the 42nd Amendment (1976), on the recommendation of the Swaran Singh Committee.

wrong.B: The 44th Amendment reversed some Emergency-era changes.
wrong.C: The 52nd Amendment added the anti-defection law.
wrong.D: The 67th Amendment is unrelated to Fundamental Duties.

21.fact: The Fundamental Duties are listed in Article 51A (originally ten, now eleven).

22.correct: Dolphins, bats and porpoises produce ultrasound (sounds above 20,000 Hz) for echolocation.

wrong.B: Whales, elephants and giraffes are associated more with low-frequency (infrasound) communication.
wrong.C: Dogs, cats and lions do not chiefly produce ultrasound.
wrong.D: Rhinos, moths and tigers are not the standard ultrasound producers listed.

22.fact: Bats and dolphins use echolocation - emitting ultrasound and sensing the echoes - to navigate and hunt.

23.correct: A pencil partly dipped in water appears bent at the surface because of refraction - light bends as it passes between water and air.

wrong.A: Reflection is the bouncing back of light, not bending.
wrong.C: Diffraction is the bending around obstacles/edges.
wrong.D: Interference is the superposition of waves.

23.fact: Refraction occurs because light travels at different speeds in water and air.

24.correct: A major post-1991 fiscal reform to strengthen revenue collection was the simplification of indirect taxes through MODVAT/VAT (moving towards a value-added tax system that reduced cascading).

wrong.A: Increasing subsidies does not strengthen revenue.
wrong.B: GST came much later (2017); the post-1991 reform referred to here is the MODVAT/VAT simplification.

24.fact: MODVAT/CENVAT and later state VAT (2005) progressively reduced the cascading of indirect taxes, culminating in GST (2017).

25.correct: In hockey, a 'slap shot' is a powerful shot taken by drawing the stick back and swinging it quickly forward to strike the ball.

wrong.A: It is not taken with an open hand.
wrong.C: It is not a wrist-only quick shot.
wrong.D: It is not specifically a short-range push.

25.fact: The slap shot generates speed while keeping the stick close to the ground.

26.correct: The Persian ruler Shah Tahmasp helped Humayun regain the Mughal throne after his exile.

wrong.A: Bahadur Shah of Gujarat was an adversary.
wrong.B: Sher Shah Suri had driven Humayun out.
wrong.D: Rana Sanga was an earlier Rajput opponent of Babur.

26.fact: With Persian support, Humayun recovered Delhi in 1555, a year before his death.

27.correct: India's first Export Processing Zone (EPZ) was set up at Kandla, Gujarat (1965).

wrong.A: Paradip was not the first EPZ.
wrong.C: Mundra was not the first EPZ.
wrong.D: Haldia was not the first EPZ.

27.fact: Kandla was also Asia's first Export Processing Zone.

28.correct: The Ministry of Law and Justice was associated with the inauguration of the 'Mahila Aarogyam Kaksh' in July 2025.

wrong.A: It was not the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare.
wrong.B: It was not the Ministry of Women and Child Development.
wrong.D: It was not the Ministry of Home Affairs.

28.fact: Women-focused facilities aim to improve access to services for women.

29.correct: The statement that loess deposits are NOT found in China is incorrect - China has the famous Loess Plateau formed by wind deposition.

wrong.A: It is true that wind is an active agent of erosion and deposition in deserts.
wrong.B: It is true that a mushroom rock has a narrow base and broad top.
wrong.D: It is true that sand dunes are examples of wind action in deserts.

29.fact: China's Loess Plateau is a classic example of wind-deposited (aeolian) loess soil.

30.correct: As per the Global Gender Gap Index 2025, India's score was about 64.1% in 2024, and the 2025 figure cited is about 64.4%.
30.fact: The Gender Gap Index measures gender parity in economics, education, health and politics.

31.correct: Article 32 of the Constitution provides the right to constitutional remedies - the right to move the Supreme Court to enforce Fundamental Rights.

wrong.A: Validation of certain Acts is a different provision.
wrong.B: Freedom to manage religious affairs is Article 26.
wrong.D: Protection of minorities' interests is Article 29.

31.fact: Dr Ambedkar called Article 32 the 'heart and soul' of the Constitution.

32.correct: Article 212 bars courts from inquiring into the proceedings of a state legislature (on grounds of procedural irregularity).

wrong.A: Article 213 deals with the Governor's ordinance power.
wrong.B: Article 211 restricts discussion of judges' conduct.
wrong.D: Article 208 deals with rules of procedure.

32.fact: A similar protection for Parliament's proceedings is given by Article 122.

33.correct: Bharatanatyam is the classical dance form that originated in Tamil Nadu.

wrong.A: Thiruvathirakali is a Kerala group dance (not a classical form here).
wrong.B: Bagurumba is an Assamese (Bodo) folk dance.
wrong.C: Veeranatyam is a Telugu temple dance.

33.fact: Bharatanatyam is one of the eight classical dance forms recognised in India.

34.correct: The traditional dances 'Seecha' and 'Kukui Kucho' are associated with Nagaland.

wrong.D: They are not from Tamil Nadu.

34.fact: Nagaland's many tribes have a rich variety of folk dances showcased at the Hornbill Festival.

35.correct: The acronym 'NCMRWF' relates to the Ministry of Earth Sciences - it is the National Centre for Medium Range Weather Forecasting.

wrong.A: It is not under the Ministry of Environment.
wrong.C: It is not under the Ministry of Cooperation.
wrong.D: It is not under the Ministry of Education.

35.fact: NCMRWF provides numerical weather-prediction support for forecasts in India.

36.correct: The 'Silyen' or 'Bubjal' used in Ladakh are types of cymbals (percussion).

wrong.D: They are not bells in the sense asked.

36.fact: Cymbals are widely used in Ladakhi Buddhist monastery (gompa) music.

37.correct: NASA's 'Europa Clipper' mission will explore Jupiter's moon Europa.

wrong.A: There is no current NASA mission simply named 'Europa Orbiter' for this.
wrong.D: JUICE is the European Space Agency's Jupiter mission, not NASA's Europa mission.

37.fact: Europa is believed to have a subsurface ocean beneath its icy crust, a target in the search for life.

38.correct: The Tyndall effect can be seen when a fine beam of light enters a room through a small hole and the path of light becomes visible due to scattering by dust particles.

wrong.B: Brownian motion is the random movement of particles in a fluid.
wrong.C: Diffraction is the bending of light around edges.
wrong.D: Deposition is a change of state from gas to solid.

38.fact: The Tyndall effect is the scattering of light by colloidal particles.

39.correct: A 'free kick' is NOT a way to score a goal in field hockey (it is a football term); hockey goals come via field goals, penalty corners and penalty strokes.

wrong.A: A field goal is a valid way to score.
wrong.B: A penalty corner can lead to a goal.
wrong.C: A penalty stroke is a way to score.

39.fact: In hockey, a goal can be scored only from within the shooting circle (the 'D').

40.correct: Khelo India Winter Games 2024 was the fourth edition in the Khelo India calendar.

wrong.A: It was not the first edition.

40.fact: The Khelo India Winter Games promote winter sports, held mainly in Ladakh and Jammu & Kashmir.
